titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Andrew J. Russell Photographs Taken During Construction of the Union Pacific Railroad
Russell (Andrew J.) Photographs Taken During Construction of the Union Pacific Railroad
titlestmt.author: Mary A. Caldera

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday April 25, 2025 at 23:25

did.unittitle: Andrew J. Russell photographs taken during construction of the Union Pacific Railroad

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 203 black and white photgraphic prints depicting the construction of the Union Pacific Railroad in Wyoming and Utah. The photographs were commissioned by the Union Pacific Railroad Company and taken by Andrew J. Russell between 1864 and 1869. Includes images of laborers, military personnel, housing, living and work conditions, as well as Shoshone men, including Chief Pocatello, Morman families, and Salt Lake City.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Andrew J. Russell (1829-1902) was an American photographer, known for his photgraphs of the American Civil War and as official photographer of the Union Pacific Railway in Wyoming and Utah. He published the Union Pacific photographs in several publications including the Union Pacific Railroad Views.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The image titles were transcribed from the captions on the back of the photographs.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Andrew J. Russell are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on the use of these materials. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Andrew J. Russell Photographs Taken During Construction of the Union Pacific Railroad. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

Shoshone Indians on the war path

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: According to Russell, the man fourth from the left is Washakie. Shoshonie Chief Pocatelllo stands on Washaskie's left.
